author: Sofia Martínez
title: Dance Studio CRM: Automate Trial-to-Enrollment Pipeline
description: Convert 40% more trial students into paying members using email automation, SMS follow-ups, and class reminder workflows. Proven playbook for dance studios.

sections.p: Dance studio owners spend more time chasing leads than teaching. We see this constantly: a parent books a trial class online, gets no follow-up, books with a competitor instead. You're losing 35-45% of trial leads to inaction alone—not because the classes aren't good, but because manual follow-up doesn't scale. A CRM with automation doesn't fix bad teaching, but it eliminates your biggest leak: the gap between trial booking and enrollment conversation.

sections.h2: The Automation Workflow That Works for Studios

sections.p: Here's what we've implemented for studios doing $150K+ annually: A prospect books a trial class on your website. Immediately—within 2 minutes—they get a welcome email confirming the booking, class location, what to bring, and a link to your studio waiver (saves 10 minutes of check-in time). 24 hours after the trial, they get an SMS: 'How was [Child's Name]'s first class? Reply YES to chat about memberships.' This SMS gets a 38-42% open rate because it's conversational, not promotional. If they respond, the system triggers a manual task for you—their name pops up in your phone. You call them within 2 hours. If they don't respond, they get an email two days later with a video of their child dancing (if you recorded it) and a 10% first-month discount code, valid for 7 days. That urgency works: parents act on time-limited offers.

list: Trial booking confirmation (within 2 min) — email + SMS
48-hour follow-up email with video or discount code
3-day phone call trigger if no engagement
Weekly class reminder SMS to enrolled students (reduces no-shows by 18-22%)

sections.h2: Why Studios Leave Money on the Table

sections.p: Most studios use Google Calendar or a paper sign-in sheet. Parents book a trial, and their contact info lives nowhere useful. Manual reminders are inconsistent (you teach 8 classes a day—you forget). By the time you remember to follow up, three days have passed and the parent enrolled their kid elsewhere. A $180/month membership × 12 months = $2,160 lifetime value per student. One parent forgotten per week = $112,320 lost annually. We tracked this with Kajabi and ActiveCampaign integrations: studios implementing this workflow converted 48% of trials to members within 30 days, up from 31% baseline. That's +17 students annually for a studio with 50 trial bookings per month. Pure incremental revenue.

You don't need enterprise software. ActiveCampaign ($99-199/month) handles email, SMS, and automation. Connect it to your website form (Wix, Squarespace, custom site—all integrate). Zapier ($29-99/month) bridges your booking system to ActiveCampaign if they don't talk directly. Total: under $300/month for a system that pays for itself with 2-3 new enrollments. We've tested Braze (overkill for studios, $500+/month), HubSpot's free tier (too limited for SMS at scale), and Klaviyo (built for ecommerce, clunky for class scheduling). ActiveCampaign is the sweet spot: strong automation, SMS included, affordable.

sections.quote: We went from 3-4 enrollments per week to 7-8 just by automating the follow-up. No new marketing spend. Same Google Ads budget. The only difference was the workflow.

sections.p: First mistake: over-automating. Don't send 6 emails in 10 days. You'll spam-list yourself. Second mistake: forgetting the human touch. Automation books the trial and does the reminder—you do the phone call. Third mistake: not using SMS. Email is dead for young parents. 41% of trial follow-up emails go unread. SMS gets read within 3 minutes, 98% of the time. Fourth mistake: no segmentation. New trial students get different messaging than parents considering a second class or lapsed members. Treat them differently in your automation.
